nameMeanings.name: Verch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Orlo.

variants.name: Ferch
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

nameMeanings.name: Madog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Madore
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Eusebe, Fernand, Lucien, Normand, Adhemar, Adrien, Donat, Elzear, Francois, Gaetan, Gilles.
French Canadian: from a French shortened form of the Spanish personal name Amador, from Latin Amator (from amator ‘lover’), a Christian saint's name. Compare Mador .
In some cases possibly also Breton (Madoré): nickname meaning literally ‘good help’, from Old Breton mat ‘good’ and uuoret ‘help’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Verch Madog was born in 1125, in Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Gronwy ap Rhydderch. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Verch Madog est née en 1125, à Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Gronwy ap Rhydderch. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Verch Madog wurde 1125 in Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit Gronwy ap Rhydderch mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Verch Madog è nata il nel 1125 nel Montgomeryshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Gronwy ap Rhydderch. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Verch Madog(은)는 1125년 영국 웨일스 몽고머이실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Gronwy ap Rhydderch(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Verch Madog родилась в 1125 г. в местности монтгомиришир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Gronwy ap Rhydderch. Она умерла в её родном городе.
